hero.label: Who We Are
hero.title: About RAPP
hero.subtitle: Rwandans Allied for Peace and Progress — a local NGO founded in Kigali in 2003, creating awareness, reducing HIV/AIDS stigma, and empowering communities through theater and income-generating activities.

story.label: Our Story
story.title: Born from Rwanda's Spirit of Resilience
paragraphs: RAPP was founded in 2003 in Kigali as a local non-governmental organization (NGO) and implements projects from different international donors. RAPP works in urban and rural districts in collaboration with partners composed of Government and National and International NGO's. RAPP recognizes the link between HIV prevention and stigma-reduction and discrimination against people who are living with HIV, and taboos related to sexual relations. RAPP creates awareness among young people about Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR), HIV/AIDS and how to prevent it using theater and other unique forms of message transportation.

Our outreach also benefits people that are infected or affected by HIV and other vulnerable and low income people financially. RAPP helps to reduce poverty by using our purchasing power to initiate and/or support business and Income Generating Activities (IGA) run by local self-help groups.

values.title: Theater for Change
values.desc: We use creative arts — theater, song, poetry — as powerful tools to educate communities and shift attitudes on HIV/AIDS and sexual health.

values.title: Health & Dignity
values.desc: We recognize the link between HIV prevention, stigma-reduction, and taboos around sexual relations, and work to address all three.

values.title: Community-Led
values.desc: Every programme is designed with — not for — the communities we serve, through self-help groups and local cooperatives.

values.title: Economic Empowerment
values.desc: We reduce poverty by using our purchasing power to initiate and support income-generating activities (IGAs) run by local self-help groups.

values.title: Equity & Inclusion
values.desc: We prioritize the most vulnerable — people living with HIV, women, youth, and low-income communities in urban and rural Rwanda.

values.title: Accountability
values.desc: We implement projects from international donors with full transparency, collaborating with government and national and international NGOs.

milestones.event: RAPP founded in Kigali as a local NGO, beginning work on HIV/AIDS awareness and SRHR education for youth through theater.
Launched the In School Project — theatre competitions and HIV/AIDS education for school youth clubs nationwide.
Started the Voices of Health Project, funded by naction medeor (Germany), reaching communities through interactive theater troupes.
Rwanda Knits expanded to 29 cooperatives, with USAID support enabling over 1,200 women to earn a sustainable income through knitting.
The Ingenzi Knit Union (IKU) formed as the country's first secondary artisan cooperative union, partnering with Indego Africa for market access.
Completed the BCSM project (USAID-funded), with 120+ peer educators trained and the Global Fund R6 project for vulnerable cooperatives.

team.name: Gedeon Bihonzi
team.bio: Bihonzi Gedeon has been the Executive Director of RAPP for two years. He has experience with various national and international NGOs. He received a Bachelor's Degree in Social Sciences.

team.name: Amini Kabano
team.bio: Amini has worked with RAPP for one year and holds a Bachelor's Degree in Accounting.

team.name: Patrick Nimubona
team.bio: Patrick has been working with RAPP for two years. He has a Bachelor's Degree in Management. He works closely with Rwanda Knits, one of RAPP's community projects.

team.name: Francine Kayigemera
team.bio: Francine has been working with RAPP for one year and has a B.A. in Business and Administration.

team.name: Sammy Kalisa
team.bio: Sammy has worked with RAPP for two years as Project Manager on the Amaijwi y'ubuzima project.

team.name: Enock Rukundo
team.bio: Enock has been working with RAPP for two years on the Voices of Health project. He holds a Bachelor's Degree in Food Science and Technology and a Post-Graduate Diploma in Enterprise Development.

team.name: Philippe Ngobonziza
team.bio: Philippe has been working with RAPP for four years, training performers in the Behavior Change and Social Market (BCSM) project. His project is Theater on HIV.

team.name: Douglas Mubirigi
team.bio: Douglas has worked with RAPP for four years. He has been working in IT since 2005 and holds a degree in Information Technology.

team.name: Rose Tuyishime
team.bio: Rose has worked with RAPP for six years. She received her Diploma in Biology & Chemistry and works with the Behavior Change and Social Market (BCSM) project.
